title: Add to Find a Longer Length
description: When a length is described as a certain number of centimeters longer than another, add the extra amount to the original length.
condition: Given a base length a and a statement that another object is b centimeters longer than it.
response: Compute a + b to find the new total length.
label: Principle / Model
example: If a crayon is 10 cm and a marker is 7 cm longer, compute $10 + 7 = 17$ cm.

title: Count Equal Units to Measure Length
description: Determine an object's length by counting the number of equal-sized units that span it.
condition: Given an object aligned with equal measurement units (e.g., centimeter cubes).
response: Count the total number of units to state the length in centimeters.
label: Production / Skill
example: If 14 cubes line up with the object, its length is 14 cm.

title: Identify Shorter or Taller Object
description: Determine which of two objects has less or greater height.
condition: Given two objects with visibly different heights.
response: State which object is shorter or taller.
label: Production / Skill
example: If the dog’s height is less than the girl’s, state: The dog is shorter than the girl.

title: Order Lengths from Shortest to Tallest
description: Arrange multiple objects or numbers in order based on their measured lengths.
condition: Given three or more objects or measurements with different lengths.
response: List them in ascending (shortest to tallest/least to greatest) order.
label: Production / Skill
example: Order 7 cm, 12 cm, 14 cm from shortest to tallest: $7, 12, 14$.
